title: Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log
summary: Focused extreme medical support for smoke inhalation mass-exposure log in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: After fires, generators, charcoal, or blocked ventilation, breathing symptoms can be deadly even without visible burns. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”.
paper and pen. Context: “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log»
steps: Move to fresh air only if safe.
Call emergency help for confusion, headache with multiple people affected, chest pain, fainting, soot, hoarse voice, or breathing trouble.
Do not re-enter the space for belongings.
Turn off fuel sources only if safe.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”.

Escalate immediately for trouble breathing, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe pain, chest pain, stroke signs, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, poisoning, or rapid worsening. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”.
Do not delay evacuation or professional care to finish a checklist. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”.
Keep privacy, consent, dignity, and local law in mind even during collapse. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”.
common_mistakes: Trying to diagnose instead of recognizing danger signs while carrying out “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
Forgetting times, medicines, allergies, and changes while carrying out “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
Touching fluids or wounds without hand hygiene while carrying out “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
Moving an injured person without a safety reason while carrying out “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
Letting panic, crowds, or rumors control care while carrying out “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
alternatives: Use certified first-aid training where available as a fallback for “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
Use official emergency services or the nearest clinic as a fallback for “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
Use a pharmacist for medicine questions as a fallback for “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
Use radio/phone consultation if transport is delayed as a fallback for “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
Use written handover notes when devices fail as a fallback for “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
failure_signs: Breathing, bleeding, consciousness, pain, fever, confusion, weakness, or skin color gets worse during “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
The helper cannot keep the scene clean or safe during “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
Transport, water, power, or communication failure blocks care during “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
The person becomes unable to drink, speak, walk, recognize people, or stay awake during “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log”
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to replace a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, poison center, or trained first-aider when using the “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log” record
Do not use it for surgery or invasive treatment when using the “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log” record
Do not use it to change prescription medicines or doses when using the “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log” record
Do not force care on a conscious adult except immediate life-safety actions allowed by local rules when using the “Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log” record
short_term: Reduce immediate danger, call or send for qualified help, keep the person clean, warm or cool as needed, and record facts without guessing. This note applies specifically to Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log.
long_term: Build certified first-aid capacity, update medical records, protect supplies, practice handovers, and map routes to pharmacies, clinics, hospitals, and shelters. This note applies specifically to Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log.
if_method_fails: Escalate by the fastest safe route: emergency number, clinic, hospital, pharmacist, poison center, radio medical consult, trained first-aider, or evacuation convoy. Record what failed and when. This note applies specifically to Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log.
environment_notes: In apartments, shelters, rubble zones, heatwaves, floods, smoke, and outages, medical problems worsen because of crowding, poor air, poor hygiene, no elevator, no refrigeration, and delayed transport. This note applies specifically to Smoke inhalation mass-exposure log.

title: Drowning after-rescue observation
summary: Focused extreme medical support for drowning after-rescue observation in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: A person rescued from water can worsen later. Breathing trouble, cough, fatigue, confusion, or chest symptoms need urgent review. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Drowning after-rescue observation”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Drowning after-rescue observation”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Drowning after-rescue observation”.
paper and pen. Context: “Drowning after-rescue observation”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Drowning after-rescue observation»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Drowning after-rescue observation»
steps: Call emergency help if breathing is abnormal, the person was unconscious, or symptoms continue.
Do not assume recovery because the person can talk.
Record water type, submersion time estimate, and symptoms.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. title: Asthma and breathing device outage plan
summary: Focused extreme medical support for asthma and breathing device outage plan in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: Breathing conditions become dangerous when dust, smoke, heat, cold, stress, or power loss removes usual support. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Asthma and breathing device outage plan”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Asthma and breathing device outage plan”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Asthma and breathing device outage plan”.
paper and pen. Context: “Asthma and breathing device outage plan”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Asthma and breathing device outage plan»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Asthma and breathing device outage plan»
steps: Keep the person’s action plan, inhalers, spacers, batteries, and device manuals together.
Reduce smoke, dust, perfume, mold, and exertion triggers.
Help the person use their own prescribed medicine exactly as labeled or as their plan says.
Escalate if speaking is hard, lips look blue/gray, breathing worsens, or usual medicine does not help.
